ops.text: Phase 1 COMPLETE — captain-hook v11.0.0 RELEASED. Sequence: pack-redesign rebased twice more (main advanced under us: a6ef362f = PEP 723 fence fix released tag-only as v10.7.0 with NO changelog heading — completed their orphaned heading inside my release commit; then 6ce27f34 plugin-sync). One modify/delete conflict each time (old-path general manifest, resolution = keep deletion; their comments.py change followed the rename into builtin_packs/general/hooks/ and their new tests pass in our tree). Pushed main → cca0be76 (11 commits, +CHANGELOG [11.0.0] + backfilled [10.7.0]); CI/Guides/Documentation all green; tagged v11.0.0; Release (PyPI) run 29704633292 success. BREAKAGE WINDOW OPEN: fleet dispatch resolves v11 via uvx and every old-layout plugin pack (ccx, cc-notes, cc-guides, cc-present, show, codex, getaway) is silently absent until Phase 2 lands — builtins fire fine. Phase 2 dispatched immediately (wf_c1efe9ec-658): 6 opus xhigh move lanes (cc-context incl. lockstep-job deletion + CI retarget; cc-notes; cc-present; cc-guides; cc-skills show+codex; getaway fold-in) each verified by an independent disk-truth opus lane running pack test itself. Codex still usage-limited — Phase 2 re-routed to opus per the escalation rule. Sidebar completed en route: project-scope self-enablements removed (cc-orchestrate fragment-sourced fix shipped c33c3e77 green; captain-hook install record removed, committed false policy restored after uninstall clobbered the rendered artifact); user decision: fleet baseline project enablements STAY (repos self-contained) — recorded in memory.
